document_summary:
Park, Choe & Veitch (ICML 2024) formalize the Linear Representation Hypothesis (LRH) — the informal idea that high-level concepts in large language models are encoded as linear directions in representation space. The paper addresses two questions left open by prior empirical work: what does 'linear representation' actually mean, and which inner product on the representation space gives semantically meaningful notions of similarity and projection. Three informal notions had circulated independently — subspace (Mikolov-style analogies), measurement (linear probing), and intervention (steering vectors / activation addition) — without a unifying account. The paper supplies that account using counterfactual pairs as the foundational object.

The core construction begins by distinguishing the two representation spaces of any softmax LM: the embedding (context) space Λ where λ(x) lives, and the unembedding (output word) space Γ where γ(y) lives, with next-token probabilities given by softmax over λ(x)^T γ(y). A concept variable W is treated as a latent binary variable, caused by context and causing output, whose value can be read off the sampled token. Two concepts are causally separable when they can vary freely and in isolation (e.g., English⇒French and male⇒female yield the four-token set {king, queen, roi, reine}, whereas English⇒French and English⇒Russian cannot co-vary). Subspace representations are then defined separately in each space via cones over counterfactual differences: γ̄_W collects γ(Y(1))−γ(Y(0)) in the unembedding space; λ̄_W collects context-pair differences whose ratio shifts P(W) but leaves P(Z|W) invariant for every causally separable Z.

Three theorems then tie these formal subspace definitions to the measurement and intervention notions. Theorem 2.2 shows the unembedding representation γ̄_W is exactly the regression direction for a logit-linear probe on the concept, making it an 'ideal' probing direction free of off-target correlation. Theorem 2.5 shows that adding c·λ̄_W to a context embedding alters the probability of the target concept without disturbing any causally separable Z, recovering steering. Lemma 2.4 is the bridge — it forces λ̄_W^T γ̄_W > 0 and λ̄_W^T γ̄_Z = 0 — and motivates the central conceptual move of the paper: a representation-space inner product under which causally separable concepts are orthogonal would unify the embedding and unembedding representations into a single object.

The second half of the paper develops this 'causal inner product'. Because the softmax is invariant to (γ, λ) → (Aγ+β, A^{-T}λ), representations are identified only up to invertible affine transformation, so no natural inner product is picked out by training and the Euclidean inner product carries no a priori semantic warrant. Definition 3.1 imposes causal orthogonality as the missing principle. Theorem 3.2 proves that any such inner product makes the Riesz isomorphism γ̄ ↦ ⟨γ̄,·⟩_C map γ̄_W to λ̄_W^T — unifying the two representations and turning the abstract construction into a concrete recipe: build steering vectors from probe directions via a single linear map. Theorem 3.4, under an independence assumption that ratios λ̄_W^T γ are uncorrelated across causally separable W on random vocabulary tokens (Assumption 3.3), gives the closed-form M = Cov(γ)^{-1} (choosing the diagonal scale D=I_d), so the causal inner product is ⟨γ̄, γ̄'⟩_C := γ̄^T Cov(γ)^{-1} γ̄' — a simple whitening of the unembedding matrix.

Experiments on LLaMA-2-7b across 27 concepts (22 from BATS 3.0, 4 language pairs, plus frequent⇒infrequent) validate the framework. Counterfactual-pair differences align with γ̄_W far more than random word pairs for 26/27 concepts (only thing⇒part fails). The estimated causal inner product makes causally separable concepts approximately orthogonal with an interpretable block-diagonal structure. γ̄_W generalizes as a linear probe on non-counterfactual Wikipedia samples (French vs Spanish). Intervention representations λ̄_W = Cov(γ)^{-1} γ̄_W steer outputs cleanly — adding α·λ̄_{male⇒female} to 'Long live the' shifts the top-1 token from 'king' to 'queen' while leaving capitalization untouched. The Euclidean inner product surprisingly works on LLaMA-2 (likely due to approximately isotropic unembedding covariance), but fails on Gemma-2B (which ties input embeddings to unembeddings), while the causal inner product remains robust on both. A vocabulary-wide scatter sanity-checks Assumption 3.3 directly. The paper explicitly leaves model parameters and intermediate-layer activations to future work.

core_concepts.definition: The conjecture that high-level concepts (language, tense, gender, capitalization, etc.) are encoded as linear directions in a language model's representation space, so that linear-algebraic operations on representations correspond to semantic operations on outputs.
relationships: splits into three previously informal notions — subspace, measurement, intervention — which this paper unifies
operationalized by counterfactual pairs that vary a single concept in isolation
validated empirically across 26 of 27 concepts in LLaMA-2-7b unembedding space

core_concepts.definition: Input/context representation space; the language model maps each context x to a vector λ(x) ∈ R^d that conditions next-token sampling.
relationships: houses the embedding representation λ̄_W and the intervention/steering directions
dual to the unembedding space Γ via the softmax P(y|x) ∝ exp(λ(x)^T γ(y))
identified with Γ via the Riesz isomorphism under the causal inner product

core_concepts.definition: Output/word representation space; each vocabulary token y maps to γ(y) ∈ R^d, and the next-token distribution is a softmax over λ(x)^T γ(y).
relationships: houses the unembedding representation γ̄_W and the probing/measurement directions
an affine space (softmax is invariant to adding a constant to all γ(y))
Cov(γ) over uniform vocabulary samples defines the causal inner product

core_concepts.definition: Latent binary variable caused by the context X and causing the output Y, with an explicit ordering (W=0 ⇒ W=1) so the sign of its representation is meaningful. Values can be read deterministically off the sampled token (e.g., 'king' ⇒ W=0 for male⇒female).
relationships: defines counterfactual outputs {Y(W=w)} that differ only in W
two concepts (W, Z) are causally separable iff Y(W=w, Z=z) is well-defined for every (w,z)
represented either in Γ (γ̄_W, ties to measurement) or in Λ (λ̄_W, ties to intervention)

core_concepts.definition: Pair of outputs (Y(0), Y(1)) that differ only in the value of one target concept W. For male⇒female: (man, woman), (king, queen), (actor, actress).
relationships: is the atomic data unit of the entire formalization
stacked into mean differences γ̃_W = (1/n) Σ [γ(Y_i(1)) − γ(Y_i(0))] to estimate γ̄_W
must be filtered to single-token words to avoid multi-token contamination

core_concepts.definition: Two concepts W and Z such that Y(W=w, Z=z) is well-defined for every (w, z) — i.e., they can vary freely and in isolation. English⇒French and male⇒female are separable (king/queen/roi/reine); English⇒French and English⇒Russian are not (cannot co-vary).
relationships: defines the orthogonality constraint of the causal inner product
operationalized via Assumption 3.3 as statistical independence of concept projections on uniform vocabulary samples
produces interpretable block-diagonal structure in inner-product heatmaps over related concepts

core_concepts.definition: Direction in unembedding space such that γ(Y(1)) − γ(Y(0)) ∈ Cone(γ̄_W) almost surely for any counterfactual pair (Y(0), Y(1)) of concept W. Captures the subspace notion in the output space.
relationships: by Theorem 2.2, doubles as a logit-linear probe direction — the 'ideal probing representation' uncontaminated by off-target correlations
maps to λ̄_W under the Riesz isomorphism of any causal inner product (Theorem 3.2)
estimated empirically as the normalized mean counterfactual difference

core_concepts.definition: Direction in embedding space such that λ_1 − λ_0 ∈ Cone(λ̄_W) for any context pair that shifts P(W) but leaves P(Z|W) invariant for every causally separable Z. Captures the subspace notion in the input space.
relationships: by Theorem 2.5, doubles as a steering vector: adding c·λ̄_W changes P(W) but not P(Z) for separable Z
characterized by Lemma 2.4: λ̄_W^T γ̄_W > 0 and λ̄_W^T γ̄_Z = 0 for separable Z
constructed in practice as λ̄_W = Cov(γ)^{-1} γ̄_W rather than via direct prompt search

core_concepts.definition: Each concept is represented as a single direction (cone) in representation space; counterfactual differences for that concept point along that direction. The earliest and most empirical form of LRH (Mikolov 2013, GloVe 2014).
relationships: formalized in two flavors: γ̄_W in Γ and λ̄_W in Λ
bridges to measurement (via Theorem 2.2) and intervention (via Theorem 2.5)
fails for thing⇒part in LLaMA-2, an empirical exception

core_concepts.definition: A concept's probability is logit-linear in the representation — a linear probe can predict it. Aligns with the probing literature (Alain & Bengio, TCAV, etc.).
relationships: Theorem 2.2 shows γ̄_W is exactly the logit-regression direction
differs from a fitted probe in that γ̄_W excludes off-target correlation
validated empirically by classifying French vs Spanish Wikipedia contexts using γ̄_{French⇒Spanish}

core_concepts.definition: A concept's value can be changed by adding a single steering vector to the representation, without altering causally separable concepts. Aligns with activation addition / steering (Turner et al., Wang et al.).
relationships: Theorem 2.5 shows λ̄_W is exactly such a steering vector
constructed in practice as Cov(γ)^{-1} γ̄_W (equation 4.1) to bypass the hard search for Definition 2.3 prompts
demonstrated by shifting top-1 from 'king' to 'queen' under α-scaled male⇒female intervention

core_concepts.definition: An inner product ⟨·,·⟩_C on Γ̄ such that ⟨γ̄_W, γ̄_Z⟩_C = 0 for every pair of causally separable concepts W, Z. The principle that picks out a semantically meaningful geometry from the affine ambiguity of representations.
relationships: unifies γ̄_W and λ̄_W via the Riesz isomorphism (Theorem 3.2): ⟨γ̄_W, ·⟩_C = λ̄_W^T
has closed form M = Cov(γ)^{-1} under Assumption 3.3 and the convention D = I_d (Theorem 3.4)
leaves d degrees of freedom in choice of D, but rules out most candidate inner products including Euclidean in general

core_concepts.definition: The softmax is invariant under (γ, λ) → (Aγ+β, A^{-T}λ) for any invertible A and any β; therefore representations are identified only up to invertible affine transformation, and no inner product is picked out by training alone.
relationships: motivates the need for an external principle (causal orthogonality) to choose an inner product
explains why Euclidean similarity has no a priori semantic warrant
frames the causal inner product as an identifiability result in causal representation learning

core_concepts.definition: The bijection γ̄ ↦ ⟨γ̄, ·⟩_C from Γ̄ to its dual induced by the causal inner product. Under it, each unembedding representation γ̄_W is mapped to the linear functional λ̄_W^T.
relationships: generalizes 'transpose' — if ⟨·,·⟩_C were Euclidean, the isomorphism would be the transpose operation
is the precise sense in which Theorem 3.2 'unifies' embedding and unembedding representations
operationalized as a single linear map M = Cov(γ)^{-1} acting on γ̄_W

core_concepts.concept: Closed-form causal inner product M = Cov(γ)^{-1}
core_concepts.definition: Under Assumption 3.3 and the convention D = I_d, the causal inner product has matrix representation M = Cov(γ)^{-1}, where Cov(γ) is the covariance of unembedding vectors over uniform vocabulary samples. Equivalently, ⟨γ̄, γ̄'⟩_C = γ̄^T Cov(γ)^{-1} γ̄'.
relationships: interpretable as whitening the unembedding matrix
in transformed coordinates (A = M^{1/2}), ḡ_W = l̄_W and Euclidean inner product equals the causal one
makes intervention representations directly constructible: λ̄_W = Cov(γ)^{-1} γ̄_W

core_concepts.definition: For causally separable W, Z and γ sampled uniformly from the vocabulary, λ̄_W^T γ and λ̄_Z^T γ are independent (uncorrelatedness suffices for Theorem 3.4).
relationships: operationalizes causal separability as something statistically measurable from the unembedding matrix
validated empirically in Appendix D.6 via scatter plots over all 32K LLaMA-2 vocab tokens
scoped to uniform vocabulary samples — natural-language correlations need not be independent

core_concepts.definition: The positive ray Cone(v) = {αv : α > 0}. Used instead of a 1-D subspace because the sign of a concept direction carries semantic content (male⇒female versus female⇒male).
relationships: defines γ̄_W and λ̄_W up to positive scaling rather than ± scaling
preserves orientation needed to make 'increase P(W=1)' a directional statement
underpins the canonical normalization γ̄_W with ⟨γ̄_W, γ̄_W⟩_C = 1

processes.name: Estimating an unembedding representation γ̄_W from counterfactual pairs
steps: Collect counterfactual pairs (y_i(0), y_i(1)) for concept W from BATS 3.0, ChatGPT-4, or a curated CSV
Filter to pairs where both words are single tokens in the target LLM vocabulary
Compute the mean difference γ̃_W = (1/n_W) Σ_i [γ(y_i(1)) − γ(y_i(0))]
Normalize using the causal inner product: γ̄_W = γ̃_W / √⟨γ̃_W, γ̃_W⟩_C
For pair-level diagnostics, use leave-one-out estimate γ̄_{W,(−i)} that excludes pair i
decision_points: Which concept ordering to fix (which value is W=1) — determines sign convention
Whether to use Euclidean or causal normalization (causal recommended for cross-concept comparison)
Whether to enforce single-token filtering strictly or accept some subword contamination
common_mistakes: Including multi-token words like 'princess' (tokenized to 'prin'+'cess') — γ(word) does not exist
Projecting pair i onto γ̄_W that was estimated using pair i (circularity); fix with LOO
Treating Euclidean cosine as semantically meaningful by default rather than verifying causal orthogonality

processes.name: Estimating the causal inner product
steps: Stream all 32K (or vocabulary-size) unembedding vectors γ from the model
Compute the empirical covariance Cov(γ) over uniform vocabulary samples
Invert: M = Cov(γ)^{-1}
Use ⟨γ̄, γ̄'⟩_C = γ̄^T M γ̄' for all similarity computations
Optionally transform via A = M^{1/2} so Euclidean tools apply on transformed reps
decision_points: Choice of D in the d-degree-of-freedom family (paper uses D = I_d for simplicity)
Whether to whiten once at the start (transformed-coordinates workflow) or apply M at every inner-product call
common_mistakes: Assuming the Euclidean inner product is the causal inner product without testing — works for LLaMA-2 by accident, fails for Gemma-2B
Ignoring the affine ambiguity and trusting raw cosine similarities across models

processes.name: Constructing an intervention/steering vector from a probe direction
steps: Estimate γ̄_W as above
Compute the embedding representation λ̄_W = Cov(γ)^{-1} γ̄_W (equation 4.1) — this is the Riesz isomorphism in action
For a chosen context x, intervene: λ_{W,α}(x) = λ(x) + α · λ̄_W (equation 4.2)
Sweep α (e.g., {0, 0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.4}) and inspect the resulting top-k tokens or logits
decision_points: Intervention strength α — too small fails to flip the concept; too large produces incoherent text
Which off-target concept Z to monitor as a sanity check for causal independence
Whether to use a hand-curated context (Table 4) or sample from natural text
common_mistakes: Trying to find prompt pairs (λ_0, λ_1) directly satisfying Definition 2.3 — usually intractable, hence the Riesz workaround
Forgetting to whiten via Cov(γ)^{-1} and using γ̄_W directly as a steering vector — wrong inner product
Reporting only top-1 token changes; meaningful concept shift is often visible across the entire top-5 even when top-1 is unchanged

processes.name: Validating linear representation for a candidate concept
steps: Estimate γ̄_W from N counterfactual pairs
Compute LOO projections ⟨γ̄_{W,(−i)}, γ(y_i(1)) − γ(y_i(0))⟩_C for each pair i
Project 100K random word-pair differences onto γ̄_W as a null baseline
Plot histograms; expect strong right skew for counterfactual pairs vs. ~zero-mean for random
Flag concepts where the two histograms overlap as having no clear linear representation (e.g., thing⇒part)
decision_points: Number of random pairs for the null distribution (paper uses 100K)
Threshold for declaring a concept 'not linearly represented'
common_mistakes: Skipping LOO and getting an inflated alignment estimate from self-projection
Using Euclidean projection instead of the causal inner product when comparing concepts in different parts of the vocabulary

processes.name: Validating the estimated causal inner product
steps: Estimate γ̄_W for all candidate concepts (27 in the paper)
Compute the inner-product heatmap |⟨γ̄_W, γ̄_Z⟩_C| over all pairs
Expect near-zero off-diagonal entries for causally separable concepts and block structure for related groups
Sanity-check Assumption 3.3 by scattering (λ̄_W^T γ, λ̄_Z^T γ) over the full vocabulary — uncorrelated for separable, correlated for non-separable (Figure 13)
Compare against the Euclidean heatmap on at least one second model (paper uses Gemma-2B)
decision_points: Which concept set is broad enough to populate a meaningful heatmap
common_mistakes: Concluding the Euclidean inner product is valid because one model (LLaMA-2) shows isotropic covariance — fails to generalize
Mixing causally separable and non-separable pairs in the same null hypothesis test

processes.name: Proof technique pattern used throughout Appendix B
steps: Write the conditional logit as λ^T · (γ(Y(1)) − γ(Y(0)))
Invoke Definition 2.1 to replace the difference with α · γ̄_W, α > 0 a.s.
If reasoning about embedding effects, substitute λ + cλ̄_W and expand
Use Lemma 2.4 to evaluate λ̄_W^T γ̄_W > 0 and λ̄_W^T γ̄_Z = 0
Use the conditioning equivalence: conditioning on W (within the quadruple) ⇔ conditioning on Y ∈ {Y(W,0), Y(W,1)}
decision_points: Whether to reduce to logits-on-pairs (cleaner) or to full softmax (more general)
common_mistakes: Skipping the conditioning equivalence and tangling joint vs conditional probabilities
Treating α as a constant rather than a function of the specific counterfactual pair

expertise_patterns.pattern: Experts distinguish embedding (Λ) and unembedding (Γ) spaces and pick the right one for the task — Γ for probing, Λ for steering — then bridge via the causal inner product.
expertise_patterns.novice_mistake: Treats 'the' representation as a single object and uses raw Euclidean cosine for both measurement and intervention.
expertise_patterns.aha_moment: Each concept has two distinct linear representations (γ̄_W in Γ, λ̄_W in Λ) that the Riesz isomorphism of the causal inner product identifies — λ̄_W = Cov(γ)^{-1} γ̄_W.

expertise_patterns.pattern: Experts ground 'concept' in counterfactual pairs that vary one factor at a time, not in fitted probe weights.
expertise_patterns.novice_mistake: Defines a concept by training a probe on natural-language text, then conflates the probe direction with 'the' concept — which absorbs off-target correlations (e.g., French text being disproportionately about men).
expertise_patterns.aha_moment: Counterfactual-pair definitions give an 'ideal' probing direction that's free of confounded correlations; a fitted probe is the contaminated version of γ̄_W.

expertise_patterns.pattern: Experts test causal separability before assuming concept directions are independent.
expertise_patterns.novice_mistake: Assumes any two concept directions should be orthogonal in Euclidean cosine; treats large overlaps as bugs.
expertise_patterns.aha_moment: Block-diagonal structure in ⟨γ̄_W, γ̄_Z⟩_C heatmaps is informative — it reflects real semantic grouping (verb morphology vs language vs capitalization) rather than noise.

expertise_patterns.pattern: Experts derive steering vectors from probe directions algebraically (λ̄_W = Cov(γ)^{-1} γ̄_W) rather than hunting for contrasting prompt pairs.
expertise_patterns.novice_mistake: Tries to find prompt pairs (λ_0, λ_1) directly satisfying Definition 2.3 — usually impossible to verify the ratio-preservation condition for all causally separable Z.
expertise_patterns.aha_moment: Theorem 3.2 says the Riesz isomorphism is the bridge: a single linear map (Cov(γ)^{-1}) turns any probe into a clean steering vector.

expertise_patterns.pattern: Experts examine the entire top-k token distribution under intervention, not just argmax.
expertise_patterns.novice_mistake: Declares failure when top-1 doesn't flip; misses that the concept direction is shifting the distribution coherently.
expertise_patterns.aha_moment: Even when 'queen' isn't top-1, the top-5 list filling with 'woman', 'lady', 'her', 'female' shows the intervention is working as designed.

expertise_patterns.pattern: Experts validate inner-product choice across multiple architectures before trusting a similarity metric.
expertise_patterns.novice_mistake: Generalizes from LLaMA-2 (where Euclidean accidentally works) to all LLMs.
expertise_patterns.aha_moment: Gemma-2B's tied embeddings break Euclidean similarity but leave the causal inner product intact — the geometry is architecture-dependent and Euclidean has no a priori warrant.

expertise_patterns.pattern: Experts recognize that affine invariance of the softmax means raw representation geometry is unidentified — and seek external principles to pick a metric.
expertise_patterns.novice_mistake: Treats Cov(γ) or Euclidean cosine as 'natural' without questioning identifiability.
expertise_patterns.aha_moment: Training picks γ and λ only up to (Aγ+β, A^{-T}λ); a principle like causal orthogonality is what restores identifiability of an inner product.

expertise_patterns.pattern: Experts use leave-one-out estimation when validating directions estimated from the same data being evaluated.
expertise_patterns.novice_mistake: Projects pair i onto γ̄_W computed from pair i, getting inflated alignment scores.
expertise_patterns.aha_moment: LOO γ̄_{W,(−i)} eliminates the circularity and exposes which concepts truly admit a linear representation (and which, like thing⇒part, don't).

temporal_evolution.paradigm: LRH observed empirically as 'word2vec analogies' — Rep(king) − Rep(man) + Rep(woman) ≈ Rep(queen) — and similar regularities in GloVe. No formal account of what 'linear representation' means or which inner product justifies cosine similarity.
temporal_evolution.change_trigger: Mikolov et al. (2013b, 2013c), Pennington et al. (2014, GloVe), Levy & Goldberg (2014) report cross-lingual and morphological analogies; BATS (Gladkova et al. 2016) consolidates them as a benchmark.

temporal_evolution.paradigm: Probing emerges as the dominant interpretability method — fit a linear classifier on top of representations to predict a concept (Alain & Bengio 2017, TCAV by Kim et al. 2018). 'Linear representation' becomes implicitly identified with 'predictable by a linear probe'.
temporal_evolution.change_trigger: Concern over what probes actually measure (Belinkov 2022) plus disentanglement work (β-VAE, Higgins 2016, 2018) raises identifiability and confounding questions.

temporal_evolution.paradigm: Geometry-of-representations and mechanistic interpretability take off — Reif, Ethayarajh, Hewitt & Manning study how syntax and hierarchy are encoded in BERT/Transformers; Elhage et al. (2021), Meng et al. (ROME 2022) start treating activations as objects to localize and edit.
temporal_evolution.change_trigger: Increased capability and accessibility of large Transformers; growing demand for interpretation and control of LLM outputs.

temporal_evolution.paradigm: Steering / activation addition emerges as a third notion: Turner et al., Wang et al., Hernandez et al., Hendel et al. (task vectors), Todd et al. (function vectors), Zou et al. (representation engineering). The three notions (subspace, probing, steering) coexist as parallel literatures without a unifying account.
temporal_evolution.change_trigger: Wang et al. (2023) characterize concepts and vector algebra in diffusion models using causal language — directly inspires the present paper's causal formalization of concepts.

temporal_evolution.paradigm: Unification: subspace, measurement, and intervention notions are formally equivalent objects bridged by the causal inner product. Embedding and unembedding representations are two views of the same concept identified by the Riesz isomorphism. The Euclidean inner product is exposed as architecturally accidental (works on LLaMA-2, fails on Gemma-2B) — a closed-form whitening M = Cov(γ)^{-1} provides the principled alternative.
temporal_evolution.change_trigger: Counterfactual-pair formalization (Wang et al. 2023 inspiration) + recognition of softmax affine unidentifiability + the choice to impose causal orthogonality as the external principle for picking an inner product.

temporal_evolution.paradigm: On LLaMA-2, Euclidean cosine accidentally captures causal separability because the unembedding covariance is approximately isotropic. This may have given prior work the false impression that Euclidean is fine.
temporal_evolution.change_trigger: Architectural shift to tied input/output embeddings in Gemma — the unembedding origin becomes semantically meaningful, isotropy is lost, and Euclidean cosine breaks. Causal inner product survives both.

key_metaphors.metaphor: Two separate representation spaces (red λ̄ for inputs, blue γ̄ for outputs) merged into a unified purple space
key_metaphors.maps_to: The causal inner product induces a linear transformation A = M^{1/2} under which embedding and unembedding representations coincide (Figure 1 of the paper).

key_metaphors.metaphor: Riesz isomorphism as the generalization of 'transpose'
key_metaphors.maps_to: If the causal inner product were Euclidean, mapping γ̄ to ⟨γ̄,·⟩ would literally be transpose; the Riesz isomorphism is the same idea for non-Euclidean inner products. It sends each γ̄_W (column) to λ̄_W^T (row).
M = Cov(γ)^{-1} 'whitens' the unembedding matrix — in transformed coordinates the covariance is the identity and Euclidean tools recover semantic geometry.

key_metaphors.metaphor: Cone instead of subspace for concept direction
key_metaphors.maps_to: Sign matters semantically (male⇒female ≠ female⇒male), so concept representations live in positive rays Cone(v), not in ± lines through the origin.
γ̄_W is the regression direction that a probe would learn if it had no off-target correlation to absorb; a fitted probe on natural text is γ̄_W plus contamination from correlated concepts.

key_metaphors.metaphor: Block-diagonal heatmap as a fingerprint of semantic grouping
key_metaphors.maps_to: When ⟨γ̄_W, γ̄_Z⟩_C is plotted across 27 concepts, the block structure reflects real linguistic groupings (verb morphology, language pairs, capitalization) — geometry mirrors semantics.
A set of d mutually causally separable concept directions {γ̄_{W_k}} spans R^d like a basis, and the causal inner product is determined by I = G^T M G.
The dot product γ̄_W^T λ(x) — a single direction in Γ used as a classifier on Λ, without ever being fit on labeled context data.

anti_patterns.name: Euclidean-cosine-by-default
anti_patterns.description: Reporting cosine similarities between representation vectors using the Euclidean inner product without checking whether causal orthogonality holds.
anti_patterns.why_wrong: The softmax is invariant under (γ, λ) → (Aγ+β, A^{-T}λ), so the Euclidean inner product is not preserved under valid reparameterizations and carries no semantic warrant. It works on LLaMA-2 only by accident (approximately isotropic unembedding covariance) and breaks on Gemma-2B where embeddings are tied.
anti_patterns.fix: Use the causal inner product ⟨γ̄, γ̄'⟩_C = γ̄^T Cov(γ)^{-1} γ̄', or equivalently whiten via A = M^{1/2} so Euclidean tools apply on transformed coordinates.

anti_patterns.name: Fitted-probe-as-concept
anti_patterns.description: Defining a concept by the weights of a linear probe fit to predict it from natural-language activations.
anti_patterns.why_wrong: A fitted probe absorbs off-target correlations present in natural text (e.g., if French text is disproportionately about men, the language probe encodes some gender). The resulting direction is not a clean representation of a single concept.
anti_patterns.fix: Use counterfactual pairs (king/queen, roi/reine, etc.) to define γ̄_W as the mean directional difference. This is the 'ideal probing representation' that Theorem 2.2 shows is equivalent to a probe — minus the contamination.

anti_patterns.name: Searching for Definition-2.3 prompt pairs directly
anti_patterns.description: Trying to find contrasting prompts (λ_0, λ_1) that shift only the target concept's marginal while leaving every causally separable concept's conditional invariant.
anti_patterns.why_wrong: Verifying the ratio-preservation condition for all causally separable concepts is intractable in practice; few prompt pairs satisfy it cleanly.
anti_patterns.fix: Construct λ̄_W algebraically as Cov(γ)^{-1} γ̄_W using Theorem 3.2 / equation (4.1) — the Riesz isomorphism does the work of finding the steering direction.

anti_patterns.name: Ignoring sign of concept direction
anti_patterns.description: Using a subspace (line through origin) instead of a cone (positive ray) to represent a concept.
anti_patterns.why_wrong: Loses the directional meaning that distinguishes male⇒female from female⇒male; you can no longer say 'add this vector to increase P(W=1)'.
anti_patterns.fix: Adopt the cone formulation Cone(v) = {αv : α > 0} and fix an ordering W=0 ⇒ W=1 for each binary concept.

anti_patterns.name: Self-projection without leave-one-out
anti_patterns.description: Estimating γ̄_W from all N counterfactual pairs and then projecting those same pairs onto γ̄_W to validate alignment.
anti_patterns.why_wrong: γ̄_W literally contains each pair's contribution, so the alignment score is inflated and uninformative — you can't tell if any individual pair would actually align.
anti_patterns.fix: Use leave-one-out γ̄_{W,(−i)} that excludes pair i when projecting pair i; if the histogram still right-skews, the concept genuinely has a linear representation.

anti_patterns.name: Generalizing inner-product behavior from a single model
anti_patterns.description: Validating an inner-product choice on one architecture and assuming it transfers to others.
anti_patterns.why_wrong: LLaMA-2 has approximately isotropic unembedding covariance, which makes Euclidean cosine accidentally work; Gemma-2B with tied embeddings breaks that property and Euclidean fails. Architecture-dependent quirks masquerade as foundational properties.
anti_patterns.fix: Test on multiple architectures (paper uses LLaMA-2 and Gemma-2B). The causal inner product is robust across both; Euclidean is not.

anti_patterns.name: Multi-token-word inclusion
anti_patterns.description: Including words like 'princess' or 'compilation' in counterfactual pairs without checking tokenization.
anti_patterns.why_wrong: Such words tokenize to multiple subword tokens (e.g., 'princess' → 'prin'+'cess'), so γ(word) doesn't exist as a single vector and the pair difference is undefined.
anti_patterns.fix: Filter to single-token words in the target LLM's vocabulary before computing differences (paper does this for all 27 concepts and BATS 3.0 pairs).

anti_patterns.name: Top-1-only evaluation of interventions
anti_patterns.description: Declaring a steering intervention a failure if it doesn't flip the argmax token.
anti_patterns.why_wrong: Concept directions shift probability mass smoothly; meaningful intervention can be visible across the top-5 distribution even when top-1 is sticky (e.g., 'queen' isn't top-1 but 'woman', 'her', 'female' all appear).
anti_patterns.fix: Inspect top-k distributions or logit-ratios for the full counterfactual quadruple (e.g., logit P(queen)/P(king) and logit P(King)/P(king) together).

anti_patterns.name: Ignoring the affine-ambiguity of representations
anti_patterns.description: Treating Cov(γ) or raw γ as canonical objects with a meaningful origin and metric.
anti_patterns.why_wrong: Softmax invariance under (γ, λ) → (Aγ+β, A^{-T}λ) means the origin of Γ and any chosen inner product are arbitrary up to invertible affine transformation; conclusions drawn from one parameterization may not survive another.
anti_patterns.fix: Explicitly impose the additional principle (causal orthogonality) needed to pick out an inner product up to the remaining d-degree-of-freedom diagonal scale, then report results either in the transformed-coordinate frame or with M explicit.

notable_quotes: high-level concepts are represented linearly as directions in some representation space
What does "linear representation" actually mean?
concepts that can vary freely of each other are represented as orthogonal vectors
Intuitively, a concept is any factor of variation that can be changed in isolation.
the unembedding representation might be viewed as an ideal probing representation
the representation γ is identified (at best) only up to some invertible affine transformation
causally separable concepts should be represented as orthogonal vectors
the Euclidean inner product is not a causal inner product if M = I_d does not satisfy (3.2) for any D
we can rule out most inner products

the differences between counterfactual pairs are substantially more aligned with γ̄_W than those between random pairs. The sole exception is thing⇒part, which does not appear to have a linear representation.
Causally separable concepts are represented approximately orthogonally under the estimated causal inner product
intervening in the male female direction raises the logit for choosing "queen" over "king" as the next word, but does not change the logit for "King" over "king"
the most likely words reflect the concept, with "queen" being top-1
we do not address interpretability of either model parameters, nor the activations of intermediate layers

The key idea in the present paper is providing formalization in terms of counterfactual pairs—this is what allows us to connect to other notions of linear representation, and to identify the inner product structure.
Our work is largely orthogonal to these, since we are attempting to define a suitable inner product (and thus, notions of similarity and projection) that respects the semantic structure of language.

Our causal inner product results may be viewed in this theme, showing that an inner product respecting semantic closeness is not identified by the usual training procedure, but that it can be picked out with a suitable assumption.
Surprisingly, the Euclidean inner product somewhat works in the LLaMA-2 model as most of the causally separable concepts are orthogonal!
This may due to some initialization or implicit regularizing effect that favors learning unembeddings with approximately isotropic covariance.
One possible reason is that the origin of the unembeddings is meaningful as the Gemma model ties the unembeddings to the token embeddings used before the transformer layers.
even when the target word "queen" does not become the most likely one, the most likely words still reflect the concept direction ("woman", "queen", "her", "female")

if we know the output is one of "king", "queen", "roi", "reine" then conditioning on W=1 is equivalent to conditioning on the output being "king" or "roi". Then, predicting whether the word is in English is equivalent to predicting whether the word is "king".

domain_vocabulary.definition: Conjecture that high-level concepts are encoded as linear directions in a language model's representation space.
Input/context representation space; context x maps to λ(x) ∈ R^d.
Output/word representation space; word y maps to γ(y) ∈ R^d. Affine (softmax invariant to constant shift).
Space of differences of unembedding vectors — a d-dimensional real vector space on which the causal inner product is defined.
P(y|x) ∝ exp(λ(x)^T γ(y)) — the language model's next-token distribution.
Latent binary variable, caused by context X and causing output Y, with an explicit ordering (W=0 ⇒ W=1).
(Y(0), Y(1)) differing only in the value of W (e.g., (king, queen) for male⇒female).
Set of outputs that differ only in the value of W; their existence formalizes what a 'concept' is.
W and Z such that Y(W=w, Z=z) is well-defined for every (w, z) — i.e., they can vary freely and in isolation.
Positive ray {αv : α > 0}, used because the sign of a concept direction is semantically meaningful.
Interpretation of LRH in which each concept is represented as a single direction (cone) in representation space.
Interpretation of LRH in which a concept's probability is logit-linear in the representation; aligns with linear probing.
Interpretation of LRH in which a concept's value can be changed by adding a steering vector to a representation; aligns with activation addition / steering.
Direction in Γ such that γ(Y(1)) − γ(Y(0)) ∈ Cone(γ̄_W) almost surely. The 'ideal probing representation'.
Direction in Λ such that λ_1 − λ_0 ∈ Cone(λ̄_W) for context pairs shifting P(W) while preserving P(Z|W) for all causally separable Z.
logit P(Y=Y(1) | Y ∈ {Y(0), Y(1)}, λ) = α · λ^T γ̄_W with α > 0 a.s. — γ̄_W is the logit-regression direction.
Adding c · λ̄_W to a context embedding changes the target concept's probability while leaving every causally separable concept's probability constant.
λ̄_W^T γ̄_W > 0 and λ̄_W^T γ̄_Z = 0 for every concept Z causally separable from W; the converse holds under a basis condition.
Softmax invariance under (γ, λ) → (Aγ+β, A^{-T}λ) for any invertible A and any β. Representations are identified only up to invertible affine transformation.
Inner product on Γ̄ such that ⟨γ̄_W, γ̄_Z⟩_C = 0 for every pair of causally separable concepts.
Under a basis condition, the Riesz isomorphism γ̄ ↦ ⟨γ̄,·⟩_C of any causal inner product sends γ̄_W to λ̄_W^T.
For causally separable W, Z and γ sampled uniformly from the vocabulary, λ̄_W^T γ and λ̄_Z^T γ are independent (uncorrelatedness suffices).

domain_vocabulary.term: Theorem 3.4 (Explicit Form of Causal Inner Product)
domain_vocabulary.definition: Under Assumption 3.3 and a basis G of mutually causally separable concept directions, M^{-1} = GG^T and G^T Cov(γ)^{-1} G = D for some diagonal D with positive entries.
Under the convention D = I_d, M = Cov(γ)^{-1} and ⟨γ̄, γ̄'⟩_C := γ̄^T Cov(γ)^{-1} γ̄'.
γ̄_W normalized so that ⟨γ̄_W, γ̄_W⟩_C = 1; uniquely picks one element of Cone(γ̄_W) once an inner product is fixed.
Bijection between a Hilbert space and its dual; here γ̄ ↦ ⟨γ̄, ·⟩_C realizes the unification γ̄_W ↦ λ̄_W^T.
G = [γ̄_{W_1}, ..., γ̄_{W_d}] — d mutually causally separable concept directions spanning R^d.
λ̄_W = Cov(γ)^{-1} γ̄_W (equation 4.1) — the steering vector built from the probe direction via the Riesz map.
λ_{C,α}(x) = λ(x) + α · λ̄_C (equation 4.2); α is the intervention strength.
Estimate of the concept direction excluding pair i, used to avoid self-projection bias when validating per-pair alignment.
Linear classifier fit on representations to predict a concept value (Alain & Bengio 2017, TCAV 2018); the measurement notion of LRH.
Vector added to a representation to change model output toward a target concept (Turner et al. 2023); the intervention notion of LRH.

Research program (Elhage 2021, Meng 2022, Hernandez 2023, Turner 2023, Zou 2023, Todd 2023, Hendel 2023) on understanding model parameters and intermediate-layer activations. Explicitly out of scope for this paper.

Research program (Higgins, Hyvarinen & Morioka, Khemakhem, Schölkopf, Moran, Wang) on identifying real-world causal/semantic structure in learned representations; this paper's causal inner product is an identifiability result in this tradition.
Characterization of latent concepts and vector algebra in diffusion models; direct inspiration for the causal formalization of concepts here.
Representations in which independent factors of variation are separated into distinct directions (Higgins 2018, β-VAE).
Direction vectors in LLM activations that represent functions or tasks rather than concepts (Todd et al. 2023).
Directions in activation space capturing an in-context task (Hendel, Geva, Globerson 2023).
Top-down approach to AI transparency via large-scale representation manipulation (Zou et al. 2023).
Early framework for concept-direction interpretability (Kim et al. 2018).
Interpretability technique mapping intermediate activations through the unembedding matrix to vocabulary logits (nostalgebraist 2020).
Covariance matrix proportional to the identity. LLaMA-2's unembedding covariance is approximately isotropic, which makes Euclidean cosine accidentally usable on that model.
Linear map A = M^{1/2} that turns Cov(γ) into the identity in transformed coordinates; corresponds to M = Cov(γ)^{-1}.

Architectural choice (used by Gemma) where input token embedding matrix and output unembedding matrix share weights — gives the unembedding origin semantic meaning and breaks Euclidean-cosine semantics.
Phenomenon where a token (e.g., 'bass', 'est') appears inside many unrelated word-tokens, polluting the meaning recoverable from γ(token).
Subword tokenization scheme used by LLaMA-2; 32K-token vocabulary.

domain_vocabulary.term: BATS 3.0 (Big Analogy Test Set, Gladkova et al. 2016)
domain_vocabulary.definition: Word-analogy benchmark covering 40 morphological and semantic relations; source of 22 of the 27 concepts used in experiments.
Publicly available bilingual lexicon for 3,564 language pairs; used to find single-token mutual top-1 translations.
Decoder-only Transformer LLM, 7B parameters, 32K-token vocabulary, 4096-dim embeddings, pre-trained on 2T SentencePiece tokens (90% English). Primary experimental testbed.
Google open LM with 2B parameters and tied input/output embeddings; used as contrastive secondary testbed showing the Euclidean inner product fails while the causal inner product still works.
Concept from BATS 3.0 (example: (bus, seats)) that empirically does NOT have a linear representation in LLaMA-2 unembedding space — the sole failure case among the 27 concepts tested.
